text: Do Consumers Really Want Agentic Commerce? > Agentic commerce is advancing rapidly through AI shopping agents, new payment protocols, and products like Google's Universal Cart and ChatGPT's Instant Checkout, but consumer demand has not matched the hype. text: A Frontier Without an Ecosystem is Not Stable > Every company is going to have to build a human capital and token capital. > Human capital: knowledge, judgment, relationships, ingenuity, and pattern recognition of its people. > Token capital is the firm's AI capability it builds and owns. > Human agency will be the driver of token capital growth. > The real opportunity is not in picking the best model but instead in building a learning loop on top of models where human capital and token capital compound. > The future of the firm is the ability to compound that learning across people and AI. > We must build a frontier ecosystem where every organization can own the learning loop, create value for themselves and for the economy around them. It is the stable equilibrium we should build together. Source:

text: Aerodrome to Launch Predictive Allocation, Bringing Prediction Market Dynamics to Liquidity Allocation Aerodrome, the largest DEX in the Base ecosystem, will launch its Predictive Allocation mechanism in July, replacing historical performance-based incentive allocation with a model that rewards participants for anticipating future liquidity demand. Users who correctly identify where liquidity will be needed next can earn a larger share of protocol revenue. The team said the mechanism combines elements of prediction markets and AMMs, directing liquidity incentives toward expected future demand rather than past trading activity. — link

text: What Do Robotics Leaderboards Tell Us About The State of Robot Learning? Evaluating generalist robot policies remains exceptionally difficult because running live robotics leaderboards involves high hardware or large-scale simulation costs and risks of benchmark hacking Consequently, there is currently no consensus on a dominant architecture, with newer Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models frequently shifting ranks alongside data-rich, older policies across different testing environments
author: admin#1742094092

text: Do consumers really want agentic commerce? Consumer adoption hasn't matched the headlines... yet Despite all the hype, consumer adoption of agentic commerce has stalled because shoppers prefer using AI for product research rather than delegating final purchases to an autonomous tool Real-world case studies, such as the scaling back of OpenAI's Instant Checkout after users consistently preferred direct merchant redirection, highlight that human shopping habits and a preference for trusted retail interfaces remain difficult to change
author: admin#1742094092

text: Replace, Integrate, Create: 3 paths Agentic Payments - which path does agentic payments actually take from here? Replace: stablecoins routing around card fees, but someone must bear liability for an agent's errors and a final, irreversible stablecoin transfer offers no recourse Integrate: stablecoins becoming the on-chain settlement layer beneath cards, already underway via Visa, Mastercard, and Stripe but exposed to regulatory fragmentation and Circle/USDC concentration risk Create: entirely new machine-to-machine transactions that no card rail could ever price, the biggest prize but gated on a verification cold-start and genuine agent autonomy The likeliest future runs all three in parallel at different speeds
author: admin#1742094092

text: How to Earn a Billion Dollars Becoming a billionaire through a startup relies on exponential mathematical growth driven by a high compounding growth rate and a large market size A startup growing at even a modest 15% monthly will multiply its revenue ~4,384x over five years To achieve this rapid growth, founders must possess deep empathy for their users and build something so incredibly good that customers organically advocate for it and tell their friends Organic ideas: the best startup concepts are often discovered counterintuitively by building cool, seemingly preposterous projects with friends to solve your own current needs, which naturally predict future market demand

text: Saylor: CEBE BPS Is the Conservative Risk Metric for Bitcoin Treasury Firms Strategy founder Michael Saylor said Common Equity Bitcoin Exposure (CEBE) BPS measures Bitcoin exposure per share after accounting for debt and preferred stock claims, making it a conservative risk metric, while Bitcoin Per Share (BPS) reflects common equity growth. He added that higher liabilities can create "amplification," causing BPS and CEBE BPS to diverge and potentially outperform or underperform Bitcoin depending on capital costs and liability structure. — link

text: CoinFund Founder: Decentralized AI Is the Counterweight to Centralized Model Control CoinFund founder Jake Brukhman said that Anthropic's compliance with U.S. AI export controls highlights the growing centralization of AI models and the increasing risk of government control. He argued that decentralized AI networks could serve as a counterweight by using distributed GPU resources for frontier model training. Brukhman cited teams including Gensyn, Prime Intellect, Pluralis and Nous Research as working on distributed training approaches, while noting that Pluralis is also exploring a business model for tokenized AI models by splitting model weights among participants. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: India Issues Over 44,000 VDA Tax Notices, Finds $104M in Undisclosed Income According to The Economic Times, India's 2026 crypto tax season is seeing stricter enforcement. Virtual digital asset (VDA) gains remain taxed at 30%, while eligible transfers are subject to a 1% tax deducted at source (TDS). Investors must report each trade, swap and disposal under Schedule VDA. The report said exchanges, custodians and wallet providers are now required to submit user-level transaction data to India's Income Tax Department for automated cross-checking against investor filings. The department has issued more than 44,000 VDA-related notices and identified over Rs 888 crore ($104 million) in undisclosed VDA income. — link

text: Highlight Clip Michael Saylor: Investors Have Lost Confidence in Ethereum On June 12, 2026, at the Bitcoin Corporate Day event, Michael Saylor discussed Bitcoin's rising market dominance. He stated that, excluding stablecoins, Bitcoin's dominance in the crypto market has climbed from around 41% in 2021 to nearly 70%. Saylor believes that market confidence in Ethereum has collapsed, and it is now locked in an intense competition with other tokens like Solana and BNB. This ongoing battle has exhausted their monetary premium, meaning these tokens will have to rely solely on their utility to survive in the future. He stated that the market evolution over the past 12 months has further established Bitcoin's position as the dominant digital monetary network, solidified its attribute as digital capital, and proven that digital credit is a viable concept. — link
